P1 NONET spirit sprites fading into the bg; P2 Voltkin not firing when a structure is REDUCED by deletion to a valid 4Sq+4Tr chain. unlock_source=user. 2-agent investigation workflow + 1-round 3-way Council (Standard) ADOPT-WITH-CHANGES (accepted) + PRIME-AUDIT. council_result: 1-round 3-way Council (Claude + Grok-ANALYST grok-4.20-reasoning + Gemini-AUDITOR gemini-2.5-pro). P1: both ADOPT (+ add warm backing glow, update make_mask.py defaults, verify live + cache). P2: both ADOPT-WITH-CHANGES, convergent: (a) single consolidated matcher pass per frame on topology-change, not a 2nd trigger (the existing loop+break already gives one-trigger/frame); (b) cause-filter to PLAYER severs only (exclude bomb/creature random-fires); keep strict topology. Accepted all. PRIME-AUDIT: the player-cause-filter + reuse-of-existing-loop+break is materially safer than the PDR's 'add BOND_SEVERED to filter'. Realized the existing break already prevents Gemini's double-fire fear.

priorities.label: S100 - Tower-Defense Phase 1a+1b vertical slice: pentagram spawner -> chewer swarm chews enemy connectors -> destroyable (strict exact-shape gating) -> potato-killable; deterministic + multiplayer-safe; procedural pencil chewer w/ amusing physics hop.
priorities.objective: Build the playable TD loop: build exactly 5 triangles in a closed pentagram -> structure comes alive as a radiating spawn-zone (+tiny VP, spawns a chewer/15s) -> chewers hop to nearest ENEMY connector + chew 5x/5s -> spawner destroyable (remove a triangle OR attach an extra shape stops it, exactly) -> chewers potato-killable. Generalizes the Voltkin creature substrate; deterministic + host-authoritative + perf-gated.
priorities.scope: See .claude/plans/2026-06-24_PDR_S100_TD_PHASE1A.md + TOWER_DEFENSE_DESIGN.md (sections 2-6). Merges design Phase 1a (sim loop + caps + determinism) with Phase 1b (real pentagram recipe + ignition decoupled from the cinematic single-slot). Reuse: CreatureType/CREATURE_CONFIGS, findNearestBondTarget(+enemyOnly), creatureVerlet, applyCreatureAttack->SEVER_BOND{cause:'creature'}, additive-optional snapshot, recipe registry, computeComplexity, gameMode.addScore. Net-new: CreatureSpawner+spawnerLifecycle, persistent-FSM gate, 5-hit chew loop, per-spawner tick poll, de-hardcode VOLTKIN_ATTACK_*/CREATURE_MAX_ACCEL, pentagram predicate, chewerRenderer (pencil + amusing physics hop), CHEW_BITE effect, spawn-zone aura, potato chewer-kill counterplay, bot spawner counterplay.
priorities.testing: tsc 0; full vitest green (1522 stay green); Voltkin regression (auto-delete@1200/DESPAWN@1140/byte-identical replay); chew-to-5-then-sever; caps (global/per-spawner/per-victim); independent Voltkin-vs-chewer counting; enemy-only targeting; spawner register/revalidate/teardown; pentagram predicate (exactly-5-cycle matches, +1 extra fails, -1 fails); two-player + rebuild; extended runChewerStress byte-identical replay (HARD GATE); host save/load mid-chew; snapshot byte-budget <~16KB; TD actions absent from CLIENT_INTENT_TYPES; old-save load empty. Perf HUD + frame-time at cap; bundle delta measured.
priorities.check_method: S100 P_TD1A DONE (commit 52d822a, pushed). Built the tower-defense vertical slice as a GENERALIZATION of the Voltkin creature substrate via a 7-layer sequential build workflow + a verification stage (38 new tests). WHAT: (1) 'chewer' CreatureType w/ persistent:true + chewHits:5 + hopSpeedMul + de-hardcoded attack/maxAccel config fields; makeCreature(config,args) w/ makeVoltkinCreature as a thin wrapper. (2) FSM persistence gate: auto-delete (:135) + forced-DESPAWN (:144) wrapped in if(!config.persistent) so Voltkin (persistent:false, lifetime 1200) is TEXTUALLY UNCHANGED; chewer stays in ATTACKING the full chewHits*CHEW_INTERVAL_TICKS, increments chewProgress, severs only on the 5th hit, no re-seek while chewProgress>0, resets only when the bond vanishes. (3) Split max-1 cap by population (sourceSpawnerId null=Voltkin vs non-null=chewer) counted INDEPENDENTLY; chewer caps 8 global / 2 per-spawner / 3 per-victim. (4) findNearestBondTarget(world,creature,enemyOnly) — chewers pass true (never eat own structure); FFA target-spread via mix32 (no RNG stream). (5) CreatureSpawner leaf + spawnerLifecycle + creatureSpawners Map on World + REGISTER/REMOVE_SPAWNER actions + host-only tick poll in main.ts (modeled on the bomb-dissipate poll, NOT game/spawner.ts wall-clock) w/ throttled re-validation (lastValidatedTick, 30t). (6) pentagram.ts predicate: EXACTLY 5 triangles in a closed 5-cycle, anchor component == that exact shape (extra attached primitive OR a removed triangle both fail -> spawner removed -> swarm+income stop). Ignition dispatches REGISTER_SPAWNER WITHOUT touching activeCinematicPlayerId; spawner recipes EXCLUDED from godlyFiredThisMatch, gated per-(player,anchor) against live creatureSpawners (rebuildable after a raid; two players can each have one). (7) Persistence: host save round-trips chewProgress/sourceSpawnerId/despawnAtTick/targetBondId (SerializedBomb precedent) but netSnapshot STRIPS them (render-trimmed wire); creatureSpawners? additive-optional + nextId advance + nullish guards; PROTOCOL_VERSION 9->10; TD actions in KNOWN_GAME_ACTION_TYPES_RECORD NOT CLIENT_INTENT_TYPES. (8) Scoring: near-zero SPAWNER_INCOME_COMPLEXITY in computeComplexity (never touches PHASE_1_WIN_SCORE=630); SPAWNER_KILL_REWARD via addScore. (9) Counterplay: applyPotatoDetonate despawns chewers in blast (owner-agnostic, sorted-id); botBrain spawner-sever priority + chewer-avoid. (10) teardownSpawners wired into all 4 teardown sites. (11) Render: chewerRenderer.ts (original child's-pencil aesthetic, oversized teeth, amusing PHYSICS-DRIVEN hop = squash-stretch + arc keyed off real motion, NOT a looping image), spawnerZoneRenderer.ts (radiating pulsed aura + 'alive' bond styling), chewBite.ts host-local effect. GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1577/1577 (92 files; 38 new TD tests) — verified MYSELF, not just the agent's report. Prod build OK. KNOWN: index chunk 584.65 KiB > 560 soft charter (R14 predicted; soft budget, no hard CI gate) -> carry-forward = code-split TD render layer (S85 pattern). Visual not headless-screenshottable (Pixi rAF + game-loop-gated sync) -> live playtest = build a pentagram. NEXT: generated pencil art (better-than-Voltkin).

priorities.label: S99 P1 - NONET spirit/owl sprites fade into the dark background (mask peaked at alpha 231, never opaque).
priorities.objective: The 3 shared-mask spirits (kami/owl-a/moss-b) show their FULL outline+wings during NONET, naturally integrated, without re-exposing owl-b's cropped flame.
priorities.scope: Regenerate public/art/nonet/spirit-mask.png with a high plateau (peak 255, large opaque core, feather only the outer rim) via make_mask.py (+ new --plateau clamp + updated defaults inset0.05/feather0.20/plateau2.0 + docstring). Leave owl-b-mask.png byte-identical. Add a subtle warm backing glow behind each spirit (sudokuOverlay.buildGuardian, concentric low-alpha ellipses 0x6b5836). OUT: re-encoding the .webm vignette; touching owl-b.
priorities.testing: alpha profile (peak 255, wings@x15%=250, corners 0, ~42% opaque core); git confirms only spirit-mask.png changed; tsc 0; vitest 1522/1522; build 557.2/560; LIVE screenshot: all 4 spirits full outlines, naturally integrated, no hard edges, owl-b flame still cropped, 0 console errors.
priorities.check_method: S99 P1 DONE. Root cause: shared spirit-mask.png peaked at alpha 231/255 (never opaque) + 81px feather blur (make_mask.py defaults inset0.06/feather0.30) ate ~93% of the frame, so kami/owl-a/moss-b (which fall back to it) faded incl. wings into the dark forest; owl-b was fine (its S97 mask reaches 255 + a hard cutoff that also crops a stray veo flame). Fix: added a --plateau clamp to make_mask.py (m.point(min(255, v*plateau)) after blur) + set defaults to inset0.05/feather0.20/plateau2.0; regenerated spirit-mask.png -> peak 255, ~42% opaque core, wings opaque to x~15%, soft feathered rim, fully-transparent corners (swept params empirically: plateau 1.7 made it 100% opaque=hard rectangle; 2.0@feather0.20 was the sweet spot). owl-b-mask.png UNTOUCHED (git-confirmed) so its flame stays cropped. Added a subtle warm backing glow (0x6b5836, alpha 0.05-0.08, concentric ellipses) behind each spirit in buildGuardian to ground the now-crisp sprite in the dusk scene (Council P1). GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1522/1522; build entry 557.2/560. LIVE-VERIFIED via __SPARK__.forceNonet + screenshot: all 4 spirits render with FULL outlines (the user's faded bottom-left owl now clearly visible), naturally integrated via glow + feathered rim, no hard rectangle, owl-b flame still cropped, 0 console errors. Committed 7db1ee1, pushed (deploy auto).

priorities.label: S99 P2 - Voltkin doesn't fire when a structure is REDUCED (by deletion) to a valid 4Sq+4Tr chain.
priorities.objective: Severing/deleting bonds DOWN to a valid Voltkin chain re-evaluates the recipe and fires (not only building UP to it); bomb/creature severs do NOT random-fire.
priorities.scope: runGodlyMatcher (godlyOrchestration.ts): the qualifying-effect condition now also accepts a PLAYER-caused BOND_SEVERED (eff.cause==='player'), so findGodlyMatch re-runs on the post-sever world. Single loop + break unchanged -> one trigger/frame (no double-fire). cause!=='player' (bomb/creature/physics/godly) excluded. Strict isolated-chain topology KEPT (relaxing reopens S48 5-blob false-positive). Host-only + once-per-match gate unchanged. Tests: build+player-sever fires, bomb/creature sever no-fire, BOND_FORMED regression, gate holds. OUT: relaxing topology (separate design decision); client-side trigger.
priorities.testing: tsc 0; vitest 1522/1522 incl 5 new godlyOrchestration tests (first to drive runGodlyMatcher); build green.
priorities.check_method: S99 P2 DONE. Root cause: the godly matcher ran ONLY on bond creation - runGodlyMatcher (the sole caller of findGodlyMatch) hard-filtered eff.kind==='BOND_FORMED'; the sever reducer (severBond.ts) emits only BOND_SEVERED/SEVER_ERASE -> deleting down to a valid 4Sq->4Tr chain never re-evaluated. (godlyFiredThisMatch gate NOT the cause; first-time passes; NONET is a different id. S97 P5 already removed the cooldown cross-block.) Fix: the qualifying condition in runGodlyMatcher now also accepts a PLAYER-caused BOND_SEVERED (eff.cause==='player' only - bomb/creature/physics/godly excluded so combat chaos can't random-fire a godly, per Council). The existing single-loop+break already caps to ONE trigger/frame (so a BOND_FORMED + a sever in the same frame can't double-fire - this dissolved Gemini's double-fire concern without a bigger refactor). voltkinPredicate is global + ignores bondPos, so any qualifying eff.pos suffices. Strict isolated-chain topology (S48 P4) KEPT - relaxing reopens the 5-blob false-positive; the user's reduced structure must end as a CLEAN isolated line (4 SQ then 4 TR, endpoints deg1, middles deg2, no stubs); ?debug=1 surfaces findLongestVoltkinPartial. New godlyOrchestration.test.ts (5 tests, FIRST to drive runGodlyMatcher - stub the Pixi-only ctx fields): player-sever fires, bomb-sever + creature-sever do NOT, BOND_FORMED regression, once-per-type gate holds. GATES: tsc 0; vitest 1522/1522; build green. Committed 807bbb6, pushed.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #nonet-sprite-fade-was-a-mask-peaking-at-231-not-255: 'sprites fade into the bg' = the shared alpha matte never reached full opacity (peak 231/255) + an 81px feather blur eating the WHOLE subject (not just the rim). Fix lives in the MASK GENERATOR, not runtime code: a plateau-clamp (multiply-then-min-255 after blur) for a flat-255 core + soft rim - you can't add back opacity a mask removed (sprite.alpha is unset; the fade is in the matte's alpha channel + a baked webm vignette). Sweep params EMPIRICALLY and measure (peak / opaque-core% / corner-transparency / wing-band alpha): too-high plateau (1.7) made it 100% opaque = hard rectangle; 2.0@feather0.20 was the sweet spot. Keep a bespoke per-asset mask (owl-b) untouched - it was cropping a flame. Verify with BOTH the numeric alpha profile AND a live screenshot (the WebGL screenshot worked this time after forceNonet + ticker.update pump).

P2 #godly-matcher-only-ran-on-bond-create-not-sever: 'X didn't fire after I DELETED down to a valid pattern' = the matcher was triggered only by BOND_FORMED effects, never by a sever, so reducing TO a valid state never re-evaluated (build-up worked, delete-down didn't). Fix = also act on the sever effect, but cause-FILTER to player-initiated only (bomb/creature severs must not random-fire a godly in combat) and REUSE the existing single-loop+break (already caps to one-trigger/frame, so no double-fire from a form+sever in the same frame - dissolved the Council's double-fire concern without a bigger refactor). Keep the strict topology gate (relaxing reopens a prior false-positive); the player's structure must end as a clean isolated chain. Wrote the FIRST test to drive runGodlyMatcher by stubbing only the ctx fields it reads (the Pixi-heavy fields are for the cinematic fn). General lesson: an event-driven matcher must listen to ALL topology mutations (create AND destroy), not just creation.

S100 P_TD1A #tower-defense-is-a-generalization-not-a-new-system: the owner's big 'tower-defense' vision (pentagram comes alive -> spawns creatures -> chew enemy connectors -> counterplay) looked like a from-scratch subsystem but was ~70% already shipped as the one-shot Voltkin creature (FSM + nearest-bond AI + Verlet hop + SEVER_BOND{cause:'creature'} + recipe matcher + additive-optional snapshot). The win was MAPPING before designing: a 14-agent design workflow (7 subsystem maps -> draft -> 5 adversarial review lenses) surfaced 6 CRITICAL latent bugs the naive version would have shipped (persistent creature instant-deleted on save/load via despawnAtTick=0; per-type godlyFiredThisMatch makes spawners one-per-match-per-type + non-rebuildable = broken in FFA; chewers untargetable w/ no kill path; Voltkin own-bond fallback makes a chewer eat its own structure; cap relaxation cross-blocks Voltkin; the 'replay test guards us' assumption was FALSE because runCreatureStress only ticked voltkin). General lessons: (a) for a big feature, run a map+design+adversarial-review workflow FIRST and let it find the latent integration bugs — cheaper than discovering them mid-build; (b) generalize the existing substrate (wrap new behavior in if(!config.persistent){...verbatim...}) so the locked path stays byte-identical and the replay-determinism guard proves it; (c) a green replay/test suite is only a guard if it actually EXERCISES the new code — extend the stress harness (runChewerStress) or it gives false confidence; (d) keep host-authoritative sim state OFF the wire (chewProgress/sourceSpawnerId host-only) and tick-deterministic (mix32, never Math.random/wall-clock) so multiplayer + replay stay consistent; (e) sequential (not parallel) build agents on a shared tree avoid file-write conflicts on hot shared files (creature.ts/world.ts). Verified tsc 0 + vitest 1577/1577 MYSELF, not on the agent's report. Carry-forward: index chunk 584.65 KiB breached the 560 soft charter (R14 predicted) -> code-split the TD render layer (S85 pattern); generated pencil art is the next pass.

P2 Voltkin: matcher now re-evaluates on player-sever (reduce DOWN to a valid chain), bomb/creature excluded, strict topology kept, 5 new tests. tsc 0, vitest 1522/1522, build 557.2/560, deploy auto. 1-round Council ADOPT + PRIME-AUDIT. Ready for /handoff.
